\boxed{Epicurus}

Epicurus sought to dispel the fear of divine wrath by arguing that the universe was not created by a Supreme Being but rather governed by natural laws, drawing on Democritus' atomism. Lucretius later expanded this idea in his work, *De Rerum Natura*, to show that natural phenomena—like the motion of atoms—explain the world without divine intervention. Both philosophers rejected the notion of a controlling deity to promote tranquility and rational understanding.
